ucs.title: [LLM] Winter Vivern (TA473 / UAC-0114) macro-borne PowerShell creating scheduled task with embedded URL/encoded payload
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Winter Vivern infection chain reported by SentinelLabs, CERT-UA and Proofpoint: a weaponised Office document or HTA spawns a shell which immediately calls schtasks.exe /create, registering a task that re-launches PowerShell with an attacker URL or base64 blob. This Office→shell→schtasks sequence inside one process tree is a near-fingerprint of the group's APERETIF / Aperetif PowerShell loader and the Polish/Ukrainian phishing waves CERT-UA tracks as UAC-0114.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=schtasks.exe Processes.process="*/create*" by _time host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process | rename Processes.* as * | where match(process, "(?i)(powershell|pwsh|invoke-webrequest|iex|downloadstring|curl|http://|https://|-enc|-encodedcommand|mshta|certutil)") | rename process as schtasks_cmd parent_process_id as shell_pid | join type=inner host shell_pid [ | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe, pwsh.exe, cmd.exe) Processes.parent_process_name IN (winword.exe, excel.exe, powerpnt.exe, outlook.exe, wscript.exe, cscript.exe, mshta.exe, hh.exe) by host Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | rename Processes.* as *, process_id as shell_pid, parent_process_name as office_parent, process as shell_cmd ] | where (lastTime - firstTime) < 600 | table _time host user office_parent shell_cmd schtasks_cmd | sort - _time
ucs.kql:
let CorrWin = 10m;
let OfficeAndScriptHosts = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","hh.exe"]);
// Stage A — Office / script-host spawning a Windows shell (macro detonation)
let StageA = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeAndScriptHosts)
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project StageATime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              OfficeParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ShellCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ShellPid = ProcessId;
// Stage B — that shell creates a scheduled task whose action carries a URL / encoded payload
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner StageA on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ShellPid
| where Timestamp between (StageATime .. StageATime + CorrWin)
// TA473 / UAC-0114 schtasks lines re-launch PS with a URL or base64 — the give-away.
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "powershell","pwsh","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","DownloadString",
    "curl","certutil","http://","https://","-enc","-EncodedCommand","mshta"
  )
| project StageATime, SchTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeParent, ShellCmd, SchTaskCmd = ProcessCommandLine
| order by SchTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let CorrWin = 10m;
let OfficeAndScriptHosts = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","hh.exe"]);
// Stage A — Office / script-host spawns a shell (4688 child of macro host)
let StageA = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentLeaf in (OfficeAndScriptHosts)
    | where ChildLeaf in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
    | project StageATime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              OfficeParent = ParentLeaf,
              ShellCmd = CommandLine,
              ShellPid = NewProcessId;
// Stage B — that shell calls schtasks /create with a URL or encoded blob
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend NewLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where NewLeaf == "schtasks.exe"
| where CommandLine has "/create"
| join kind=inner StageA on Computer, $left.ProcessId == $right.ShellPid
| where TimeGenerated between (StageATime .. StageATime + CorrWin)
| where CommandLine has_any (
    "powershell","pwsh","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","DownloadString",
    "curl","certutil","http://","https://","-enc","-EncodedCommand","mshta"
  )
| project StageATime, SchTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          OfficeParent, ShellCmd, SchTaskCmd = CommandLine
| order by SchTime desc
ucs.rationale: This is not a generic 'Office spawns PowerShell' rule — it requires (1) a script-host macro/HTA detonation as parent, (2) a child shell, AND (3) that exact shell child instance launching schtasks /create within 10 minutes carrying a URL or -EncodedCommand. SentinelOne's APERETIF write-up, CERT-UA's UAC-0114 advisories and Proofpoint's TA473 reporting all describe this same three-step payload-stage

ucs.title: [LLM] Winter Vivern lookalike-domain egress from PowerShell / LOLBins (gov / MFA / OSCE typosquat C2)
ucs.description: Surface PowerShell, curl, certutil, mshta or BITS reaching out to domains that impersonate European government, ministry-of-foreign-affairs or NGO branding (e.g. ocse[.]com, poczta-mil[.]com, mod-gov[.]uk, mfa-gov[.]net) on non-government TLDs. Winter Vivern's 2021-2024 campaigns are catalogued (DomainTools, SentinelLabs, ESET) for registering exactly this style of typosquat as both staging and C2 infrastructure for their APERETIF/AMETHYST loaders.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest_ip values(Web.url) as urls from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.user_agent_length>0 (Web.process IN ("*\\powershell.exe","*\\pwsh.exe","*\\curl.exe","*\\certutil.exe","*\\mshta.exe","*\\bitsadmin.exe","*\\wscript.exe","*\\cscript.exe")) by host Web.user Web.process Web.url Web.dest | rename Web.* as * | rex field=url "https?://(?<fqdn>[^/]+)/" | where match(fqdn, "(?i)(gov|mfa|mod|mil|ministry|embassy|council|europa|ocse|osce|poczta|ukraine|polska|poland)") AND NOT match(fqdn, "(?i)(\.gov($|\.[a-z]{2,3}$)|\.mil$|\.mod\.uk$|europa\.eu$)") | stats min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(host) as hosts dc(host) as host_count values(user) as users values(process) as tools values(url) as sample_urls by fqdn | sort - lastSeen
ucs.kql:
let GovKeywords = dynamic(["gov","mfa","mod","mil","ministry","embassy","council","europa","ocse","osce","poczta","ukraine","polska","poland"]);
let LegitGovTLDPattern = @"(?i)(\.gov($|\.[a-z]{2,3}($|/))|\.mil($|/)|\.mod\.uk($|/)|europa\.eu($|/))";
let LolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","mshta.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LolBins)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| extend Fqdn = tolower(tostring(extract(@"https?://([^/]+)/?", 1, RemoteUrl)))
| where isnotempty(Fqdn)
| where Fqdn has_any (GovKeywords)
| where not(RemoteUrl matches regex LegitGovTLDPattern)
// Anti-baseline: brand-new contact in the last 24h
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            Hosts = make_set(DeviceName, 25),
            HostCount = dcount(DeviceName),
            Tools = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            SampleUrl = any(RemoteUrl),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
            by Fqdn, RemoteIP
| where FirstSeen > ago(24h)
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let GovKeywords = dynamic(["gov","mfa","mod","mil","ministry","embassy","council","europa","ocse","osce","poczta","ukraine","polska","poland"]);
let LegitGovTLDPattern = @"(?i)(\.gov($|\.[a-z]{2,3}($|/))|\.mil($|/)|\.mod\.uk($|/)|europa\.eu($|/))";
let LolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","mshta.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
ImWebSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| extend ToolLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ToolLeaf in (LolBins)
| where isnotempty(Url)
| extend Fqdn = tolower(coalesce(HttpHostName, tostring(extract(@"https?://([^/]+)/?", 1, Url))))
| where isnotempty(Fqdn)
| where Fqdn has_any (GovKeywords)
| where not(Url matches regex LegitGovTLDPattern)
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            Hosts = make_set(DvcHostname, 25),
            HostCount = dcount(DvcHostname),
            Tools = make_set(ToolLeaf, 10),
            SampleUrl = any(Url),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by Fqdn, DstIpAddr
| where FirstSeen > ago(24h)
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Winter Vivern's distinguishing operational tradecraft (per DomainTools 2021, SentinelLabs 2023, ESET 2023 reporting) is procuring lookalike domains that imitate the exact victim ministries, embassies, OSCE, the European Council, and Polish/Ukrainian government mail systems — then using them as both phishing pretexts and as PowerShell-loader C2. Constraining the source process to Windows LOLBins an

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
